evals.prompt: Turn everything we know about the billing retry flow into a PRD using the conversation and repo context; only ask if something is blocking.
evals.expected_behavior: Synthesizes a PRD from existing context rather than interviewing broadly; covers problem, solution, user stories, implementation and testing decisions, and out of scope, grounded in repository vocabulary.
assertions: Starts from existing conversation or repository context rather than a broad interview
Covers user-facing problem/solution and user stories
Includes implementation and testing decisions without file paths or code snippets

evals.prompt: Synthesize this feature discussion into a PRD and publish it to the issue tracker if labels and templates are known.
evals.expected_behavior: Produces a complete PRD markdown artifact and only publishes with verified tracker conventions; otherwise surfaces a publication blocker.
assertions: Produces or outlines a full PRD artifact suitable for issue-tracker handoff
Avoids inventing issue labels or templates that are not verified
Mentions publish-or-blocker decision rather than assuming tracker fields

evals.prompt: Break this approved PRD into independently grabbable implementation issues.
evals.expected_behavior: Routes away from to-prd because the PRD already exists and the task is issue slicing.
assertions: Does not re-author a PRD when the task is ticket breakdown
Routes toward to-issues or issue slicing instead
